{
  "tier": "field",
  "salvageable": false,
  "improvement_directive": "",
  "flagged_conjecture_labels": [],
  "critique": "The delivered result is a rate-level minimax statement on exact known-schedule shells \(q_d(P)=r_n\), with the converse further restricted to vanishing eventually capped schedules, least-favorable-compatible primitives, and \(c_G\\le3/16\). The upper bound requires direction-specific Hölder, overlap, design-density, information, and sufficient nuisance-rate conditions; it does not establish a complete rarity-smoothness frontier or necessity of that learning regime, and the propensity-only construction explicitly shows those two-nuisance conditions are not necessary. The bounded-count theorem proves only a nonshrinking lower obstruction on the smoother comparison shell, without a matching procedure or a Poisson-transition limit experiment. The TL;DR and project framing accurately limit the headline to rate matching on the admissible identical Gaussian shells and a bounded-count obstruction, rather than claiming sharp constants or a general transition theorem.",
  "flagship_potential": false,
  "flagship_directive": ""
}
